mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear periods with a t-stat greater than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint quartile portfolios show no alpha relative to market, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and business investment, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative returns during expansion phases, or the predictive power vanishes when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market strength and wage growth, supporting consumer expenditure and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, monetary policy restricts liquidity and raises borrowing costs for corporations, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the rate threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand, boosting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign during high-volatility regimes, or combined high-quartile portfolios underperform low-quartile portfolios by more than 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a recessionary phase reflects collapsing business activity and reduced capital expenditure, which forces earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns during NBER-defined recessions, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
